_comment: Park factors for WWOBA League - VOS Evaluation
_comment_2: This file contains park adjustments for evaluating players in the WWOBA organization
_comment_3: Each team should have its own park factors file (e.g., park-factors-{team-code}.json)

raw_park_factors._comment: Raw park factors from OOTP - 1.0 = league average

park_profile.description: Spacious dimensions that suppress home runs but heavily reward doubles and triples. LHB get a batting average boost (+6%). Classic gap power park.
key_characteristics: Slight batting average boost (+2.1% overall, +6% LHB)
Minimal RHB vs LHB HR split (~3%)

tool_adjustments._comment: Multipliers applied to tool ratings. 1.0 = no change, >1.0 = more valuable, <1.0 = less valuable
tool_adjustments._comment_2: These are calculated from raw park factors using conversion formulas

defense._comment: Spacious park increases value of range, especially in outfield
defense._rationale_OFR: High 2B/3B rates indicate large outfield dimensions - range is critical
defense._rationale_IFR: More balls in play (higher avg) means slightly more value to IF range

baserunning._comment: High doubles/triples rates reward speed and baserunning aggression
baserunning._rationale_Speed: More XBH opportunities mean speed is more valuable
baserunning._rationale_Run: High triples rate rewards smart baserunning decisions

pitcher_ability._comment: Park characteristics affect which pitcher skills are most valuable
pitcher_ability._rationale_Movement: Movement helps prevent gap power damage in spacious park
pitcher_ability._rationale_Control: Limiting baserunners is slightly more important in big park
pitcher_ability._rationale_HR_Avoid: Park already suppresses HRs - HR/FB specialists get less differential value

handedness_splits._comment: Optional handedness-specific adjustments - only used if use_handedness_splits is true
handedness_splits._comment_2: RHB HR 0.980 vs LHB HR 1.010 - minimal difference (~3%)
handedness_splits._rationale: Splits are minimal - not worth the added complexity

application_rules._comment: Rules for when and how aggressively to apply these park factors
application_rules._comment_prospects: Apply to all players in WWOBA organization, including minors
application_rules._comment_major_leaguers: Apply to players at ML level with this team
application_rules._comment_handedness: Disabled by default - minimal difference between RHB/LHB
application_rules._comment_strength: Use 50% strength as conservative starting point. 1.0 = full strength, 0.5 = half strength
application_rules._comment_strength_2: At 0.5 strength: Gap becomes 1.123 (12.3% boost) instead of 1.246 (24.6% boost)

validation_examples._comment: Test cases to verify park factors are working correctly

power_hitter_vs_gap_hitter.description: Compare 80 Pow / 45 Gap player vs 45 Pow / 80 Gap player

player_a_power_hitter.impact: Power slightly devalued in HR-neutral park

player_b_gap_hitter.impact: Gap power highly valued in doubles/triples park

power_hitter_vs_gap_hitter.result: Gap hitter gains significantly from park factors in this environment

center_fielder_evaluation.description: CF with elite range gets boosted

elite_range_cf.with_park_half_strength: Defense score based on 75 × 1.04 = 78 effective OFR
elite_range_cf.impact: +3 points to range rating = more defensive value

usage_notes.creating_files_for_other_teams: Copy this file and adjust raw_park_factors and team_info sections
usage_notes.tuning_adjustment_strength: Start at 0.5, increase to 0.7-1.0 if you want more aggressive park effects
usage_notes.when_to_enable_handedness_splits: Only if RHB/LHB HR factors differ by >10% (yours differ by ~3%)
